perceivable.name: Perceivable
perceivable.description: Information and user interface components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive

guidelines.name: Non-text Content
guidelines.description: All non-text content has a text alternative
examples: Icon buttons must have aria-label or aria-labelledby
code_checks: button elements with only icons have aria-label

guidelines.name: Info and Relationships
guidelines.description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined
examples: Use semantic HTML (header, nav, main, footer)
Headings follow logical hierarchy (h1 > h2 > h3)
Tables use proper table markup with th and scope

guidelines.name: Contrast (Minimum)
guidelines.description: Text has contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text)
examples: Large text (18pt+ or 14pt+ bold): 3:1 contrast ratio
Use tools to verify: WebAIM Contrast Checker
code_checks: Verify contrast for all text states (hover, focus, disabled)

guidelines.name: Non-text Contrast
guidelines.description: UI components and graphical objects have contrast ratio of at least 3:1

operable.name: Operable
operable.description: User interface components and navigation must be operable

guidelines.name: Keyboard
examples: Arrow keys for radio groups and menus

guidelines.name: Focus Order
guidelines.description: Focusable components receive focus in logical order
examples: Use tabindex=-1 for programmatic focus only

guidelines.name: Focus Visible
examples: Never use outline: none without replacement
code_checks: All interactive elements show focus state

guidelines.name: Target Size
guidelines.description: Touch targets are at least 44x44 CSS pixels
examples: Add padding to increase clickable area

understandable.name: Understandable
understandable.description: Information and operation of user interface must be understandable

guidelines.name: On Focus
guidelines.description: Focus does not trigger unexpected context changes
examples: Don't navigate away when element receives focus

guidelines.name: Error Identification
guidelines.description: Errors are clearly identified and described
examples: Error messages appear near the field

guidelines.name: Labels or Instructions
guidelines.description: Labels or instructions provided when input required
examples: Every form field has a label

robust.name: Robust
robust.description: Content must be robust enough to work with assistive technologies

guidelines.name: Name, Role, Value
guidelines.description: All UI components have accessible name and role
examples: ARIA roles when semantic HTML insufficient

quick_checklist: Color is not the only visual means of conveying information
Error messages are clear and specific
Content is organized with semantic HTML
